reforms.description: The Technology Reinvestment Project allocated $202 million towards flat-panel display research and development, aiming to enhance domestic manufacturing capacity in this technology area.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing domestic manufacturing capacity in technology is expected to raise productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The project has been successfully implemented as of 1994.
reforms.importance_rationale: The allocation of $202 million represents a significant but narrower funding initiative.
reforms.source_quote: The Technology Reinvestment Project gave some $202 million toward flat-panel display research and development in October 1994.

reforms.description: The 'Goals 2000' initiative and the 'School-to-Work Opportunities Act' aim to improve the quality of the education system and enhance workforce skills by establishing national standards and providing seed money for vocational education improvements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education and vocational skills is expected to enhance the overall skill level of the workforce, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Both initiatives have been successfully implemented as of 1994.
reforms.importance_rationale: These initiatives represent a major reform in the education system aimed at workforce development.
reforms.source_quote: Goals 2000 seeks to improve the quality of the education system in a variety of ways, including the creation of 'voluntary' national standards for initial education and for vocational skills.
